Okay, welcome back to section four. This is where we finally get to write the moment of highest tension. We already spent so much time going into that from different angles back in module two, and this is where you get to take those raw materials and write the official version. So I do want to remind you of what you are coaching in this section, however. So what you're coaching is intimacy with the symptoms of the problem. You want them to almost re-feel it and rekindle those emotions, you know, how it shows up, how it affects them, how it affects other people in their lives, and what will happen if they allow it, keyword allow it to continue. You're also continuing to build up trust by accurately reflecting back that moment of highest tension. You know, just the act of reflecting back something they're experiencing, something that they've held as this dark secret that they probably have no one in their life to talk about, that makes you a very key person in their life and someone they're willing to trust to go all the way in solving it. You're continuing to ramp up that discord, that disharmony, that discomfort with their current situation. You're making it not okay for that to continue to happen, and when you do so, you're essentially creating friction, friction, sorry, to overcome the inertia. And this is really what we're going for in this section. You want them to have no way to go backwards into feeling this. You want the discomfort of it to be so much that it creates this catalyst to move forward into the next section, which will be the moment of highest pleasure. But before we go there, I really want you to write the official version of your moment of highest tension. As a reminder, you're going to source it from the Launch Bible workbook in section three. You're going to be able to access it also from the 67 Golden Questions in section 10, as well as the Coach Mining Enrollment Extractor workbook in section one. So how do you know you're choosing the right one? Out of all the possible moment of highest tensions, I like to use this as the litmus test. You found the right moment of highest tension when you feel so viscerally ill and desperate to solve the problem as if it were your own. And the words almost just kind of fill the page from there because you almost want to get to the point where you can solve that problem. That's how you know you've stumbled upon the right moment of highest tension. And the second test to know you've written it well is does it pass the Nicholas Cage test? Now, Nicholas Cage is obviously known as an extremely talented or awful actor. However, you choose to watch Nicholas Cage movies. But yeah, he's known for being a little overdramatic. And there's this whole YouTube compilation of Nick Cage freekits, which could be part of your 10 excels pages approved procrastination. But what I'm getting at here is your copy needs to be so hyper specific and vivid and dimensional here that even if someone like Nick Cage was given the script, it is actor proof. It is Nick Cage proof. They would be able to act to act it instead of just having mindless freakouts that no one really believes. So when you are writing this, try to create a very clear mental picture of the problem play out. You know, who is present? What is happening? What does it sound like? It's smell like bring as many senses as you can and give your prospect the ability to really immerse themselves in that scene. So this all comes down to this fact, the less your prospect has to think, the more they're allowed to feel. And that's what we want to offer them here. There are times where it's okay for them to think, but in the moment of highest tension or moment of highest pleasure, which are really the emotional anchors that create that gap or chasm that must be filled, we need to do the work for them and just allow them to feel it. So if you need more inspiration before going into this, I created an example breakdown video in the next one. If you are feeling well enough, well steeped enough in your prospects moment of highest tension, I'd prefer that you go ahead, stop this video and just go ahead and write it and then meet me in the next module where we will talk about the moment of highest pleasure.
